response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with ~15+ years of experience, a CS degree, and genuine strengths in software craftsmanship, testing, and polyglot development. However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position centered on experimentation systems, growth engineering, telemetry, and metrics platforms — areas where the resume shows essentially no direct experience. There is no evidence of building experimentation frameworks, feature-flagging systems, statistical analysis workflows, or leading multi-workstream initiatives at principal scope. Career trajectory (Senior/Staff at small-to-mid-size companies) does not demonstrate the cross-org technical leadership this level requires.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and backend systems
High-throughput distributed systems exposure (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source authorship, and teaching/mentoring via workshops
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience
No demonstrated telemetry/metrics infrastructure or statistical analysis background
No evidence of principal-level scope — leading multi-team initiatives or platform adoption across an organization

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and scale exposure
